interaction_phrase: partnership to develop modular quantum supercomputer
occurrence_sentence: €21 million in funding supports Forschungszentrum Jülich-Eleqtron partnership to develop modular quantum supercomputer
€21 Million in Funding Supports Forschungszentrum Jülich, Eleqtron Partnership to Develop Modular Quantum Supercomputer
The Siegen-based quantum computer pioneer eleQtron and Forschungszentrum Jülich's world-leading supercomputing centre are entering into a strategic technology partnership.

As part of the now contractually agreed EPIQ project, a development partnership for a trapped-ion quantum computer in North Rhine-Westphalia (NRW), eleQtron is developing a trapped-ion quantum computer by the end of 2024, which will be closely integrated into the high-performance computing infrastructure of the Jülich Supercomputing Centre (JSC).
The Siegen-based quantum computer pioneer eleQtron and Forschungszentrum Jülich's world-leading supercomputing centre are entering into a strategic technology partnership.

As part of the now contractually agreed EPIQ project, a development partnership for a trapped-ion quantum computer in North Rhine-Westphalia (NRW), eleQtron is developing a trapped-ion quantum computer by the end of 2024, which will be closely integrated into the high-performance computing infrastructure of the Jülich Supercomputing Centre (JSC).

We owe this to the exemplary success story of the University of Siegen, its spin-off with eleQtron and the close cooperation with Forschungszentrum Jülich: a real beacon project for North Rhine-Westphalia as a centre of science.

interaction_phrase: partnership with the renowned Forschungszentrum Jülich
occurrence_sentence: The partnership with the renowned Forschungszentrum Jülich and the delivery of our quantum computer are a major milestone for eleQtron.

interaction_phrase: together with a start-up from North Rhine-Westphalia
occurrence_sentence: Developing a quantum computer and putting it into application together with a start-up from North Rhine-Westphalia demonstrates NRW's capabilities as a high-tech hub in Europe and also the effectiveness of the 'EIN Quantum NRW' network, through which the development partnership came about.

Siegen-based start-up eleQtron manufactures quantum computers like no other in the world. Its two founders, Prof. Dr. Christof Wunderlich and Dr. habil. Michael Johanning, were involved in the construction of the first German quantum computer.

"Developing a quantum computer and putting it into application together with a start-up from North Rhine-Westphalia demonstrates NRW's capabilities as a high-tech hub in Europe and also the effectiveness of the 'EIN Quantum NRW' network, through which the development partnership came about."

Since 2015, Forschungszentrum Jülich has set itself the goal of establishing a leading development and user community from industry and science for the application of quantum computers in Germany and Europe.

Siegen-based start-up eleQtron manufactures quantum computers like no other in the world. Its two founders, Prof. Dr. Christof Wunderlich and Dr. habil. Michael Johanning, were involved in the construction of the first German quantum computer.

interaction_phrase: to be expanded and installed in the EPIQ project by eleQtron
occurrence_sentence: The first commercial gate-based system that JUNIQ aims to offer is to be expanded and installed in the EPIQ project by eleQtron.

interaction_phrase: eleQtron will set up a pilot system
occurrence_sentence: In the first stage, eleQtron will set up a pilot system with the room temperature QPU (quantum processing unit) "HiQ" with up to 30 trapped-ion qubits by the end of this year and will install it for integration with the Jülich supercomputer JURECA DC over the course of next year.

interaction_phrase: eleQtron will expand the pilot system
occurrence_sentence: In the second stage, eleQtron will expand the pilot system into an end-user system with the cryo-QPU "HiQ+" with up to 60 qubits and install it at Jülich in 2026.

interaction_phrase: establishing a leading development and user community from industry and science for the application of quantum computers in Germany
occurrence_sentence: Since 2015, Forschungszentrum Jülich has set itself the goal of establishing a leading development and user community from industry and science for the application of quantum computers in Germany and Europe.

interaction_phrase: set up a pilot system with the room temperature QPU
occurrence_sentence: In the first stage, eleQtron will set up a pilot system with the room temperature QPU (quantum processing unit) "HiQ" with up to 30 trapped-ion qubits by the end of this year and will install it for integration with the Jülich supercomputer JURECA DC over the course of next year.

interaction_phrase: expand the pilot system into an end-user system
occurrence_sentence: In the second stage, eleQtron will expand the pilot system into an end-user system with the cryo-QPU "HiQ+" with up to 60 qubits and install it at Jülich in 2026.
